mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman correlation between INDPRO monthly change and 20-day SPX forward returns turns negative or rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment rate exceeds 9% and rises month-over-month, labor market deterioration reduces consumption expectations and compresses earnings multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances during the backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate indicate aggressive monetary tightening against inflation, which sharply elevates discount rates and suppresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS do not produce significantly lower 20-day SPX returns than either independent signal alone over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery, increases in retail sales signal consumer demand normalization that drives corporate revenue beats and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:post-2009 recovery with depressed consumer spending
failure_condition: In post-2009 recovery samples, month-over-month RSAFS growth fails to positively predict 20-day SPX returns with rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Short-term federal funds rate decreases compress the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Federal funds rate monthly changes show negative correlation with 5-day SPX forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 700 thousand units during economic weakness, further declines signal intensifying credit contraction and construction layoffs, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of observations when HOUST is below 700.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration where payroll employment declines while unemployment rises creates a double-confirmation of labor market stress that depresses consumption forecasts and equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined PAYEMS decline and UNRATE increase fail to underperform either signal in isolation for predicting 60-day SPX returns over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales provides high-frequency demand signals that are rapidly priced into equities, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly growth rate exhibits negative rank IC versus 5-day SPX forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
